topics_flat: Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Recurrence relations
Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Sums and products
prompt: Let $a_0, a_1, a_2, \dots, a_n, \dots$ be a sequence of numbers satisfying $(3 - a_{n+1}) \cdot (6 + a_n) = 18$, and $a_0 = 3$. Then $\sum_{i=0}^{n} \frac{1}{a_i}$ equals ________.
response_text:
We use the recurrence
\[
(3-a_{n+1})(6+a_n)=18,\qquad a_0=3.
\]

We want
\[
S_n=\sum_{i=0}^n \frac1{a_i}.
\]

## 1) Rewrite the recurrence
From
\[
(3-a_{n+1})(6+a_n)=18
\]
we get
\[
3-a_{n+1}=\frac{18}{6+a_n}.
\]
So
\[
a_{n+1}=3-\frac{18}{6+a_n}
=\frac{3(6+a_n)-18}{6+a_n}
=\frac{3a_n}{a_n+6}.
\]

Hence
\[
a_{n+1}=\frac{3a_n}{a_n+6}.
\]

Now invert:
\[
\frac1{a_{n+1}}=\frac{a_n+6}{3a_n}
=\frac13+\frac{2}{a_n}.
\]

Let
\[
b_n=\frac1{a_n}.
\]
Then
\[
b_{n+1}=2b_n+\frac13.
\]

Also
\[
b_0=\frac1{a_0}=\frac13.
\]

## 2) Solve for \(b_n\)
Compute a few terms:
\[
b_1=2\cdot \frac13+\frac13=1,\quad
b_2=2\cdot 1+\frac13=\frac73.
\]
This suggests solving the linear recurrence.

Set
\[
b_n+\frac13=c_n.
\]
Then
\[
c_{n+1}=b_{n+1}+\frac13=2b_n+\frac13+\frac13=2\left(b_n+\frac13\right)=2c_n.
\]
And
\[
c_0=b_0+\frac13=\frac13+\frac13=\frac23.
\]
Thus
\[
c_n=\frac23\cdot 2^n=\frac{2^{n+1}}3.
\]
So
\[
b_n=c_n-\frac13=\frac{2^{n+1}-1}{3}.
\]

Therefore
\[
\frac1{a_n}=\frac{2^{n+1}-1}{3}.
\]

## 3) Sum
\[
S_n=\sum_{i=0}^n \frac1{a_i}
=\sum_{i=0}^n \frac{2^{i+1}-1}{3}
=\frac13\left(\sum_{i=0}^n 2^{i+1}-\sum_{i=0}^n 1\right).
\]
Now
\[
\sum_{i=0}^n 2^{i+1}=2^{n+2}-2,
\qquad
\sum_{i=0}^n 1=n+1.
\]
Hence
\[
S_n=\frac13\left((2^{n+2}-2)-(n+1)\right)
=\frac{2^{n+2}-n-3}{3}.
\]

Final answer: \(\displaystyle \frac{2^{n+2}-n-3}{3}\)
